mnemonics: S.E.R.V.L.E.T. - Sun's Extensible Request/Response Vehicle for Linking to Every Technology (A fun backronym to remember Servlet's role as a web component that handles requests and responses.)
keyPoints: A Servlet is a Java class that extends `HttpServlet` and handles HTTP requests (like `doGet()` and `doPost()`).
Servlets run inside a Servlet container (e.g., Tomcat) which manages their lifecycle.
The `web.xml` file (or annotations) is used to map a URL to a specific Servlet class.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to call `super.doGet(req, resp)` or `super.doPost(req, resp)` when overriding these methods, which can break default HTTP handling.
Not using `RequestDispatcher` correctly to forward requests or include content from other resources, leading to errors or blank pages.
Mixing up the roles of `sendRedirect` (client-side redirect) and `RequestDispatcher.forward` (server-side forward).
